title: Fix recque_web: broken SessionManager import + default to claude_cli backend
description: recque_web/app.py imports recque_tui.domain.journey.SessionManager which was deleted (replaced by application.session_service.SessionService). The web app fails to import. Swap to SessionService (drop-in: signatures match) and default the web app to RECQUE_BACKEND=claude_cli so AI calls use the claude -p CLI under OAuth.
notes: Fixed: (1) SessionManager->SessionService import swap (drop-in, signatures match). (2) Defaulted web app to RECQUE_BACKEND=claude_cli via os.environ.setdefault at top of app.py (before recque_tui imports; get_config caches a singleton). (3) Found + fixed a cross-session SQLAlchemy bug: complete_session/resume_session loaded the session from a foreign factory() db, so the service committed its own empty session and the status change was lost. Now share one db session (SessionService(db) borrow pattern). Verified: claude_cli generates skillmap+questions end-to-end via HTTP; pop/save_progress HTTP 200; complete/resume now persist (active->completed->active). 113 tests pass, ruff clean. NOTE: web app depends on uncommitted question_engine.py (LearningContext), models.py (explanation field), mock_generator.py changes.

title: Add claude -p CLI backend to AIClient for dev-mode use of Max subscription
description:
Currently AIClient picks between 'anthropic' (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), 'openai' (needs OPENAI_API_KEY), and 'mock'. Both API paths cost per-token. Add a fourth backend, 'claude_cli', that shells out to the locally-installed 'claude -p' CLI — which uses the user's already-authenticated Claude Code session (drawing from the Max subscription, or post-2026-06-15 from the new Agent SDK credit pool). Lets the user run recque in dev without paying per-token.

Background — Anthropic docs (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview) state:
> Starting June 15, 2026, Agent SDK and 'claude -p' usage on subscription plans will draw from a new monthly Agent SDK credit, separate from your interactive usage limits.

So 'claude -p' is the right tool: single-shot, non-interactive, uses Claude Code auth. Until 2026-06-15 it draws from the interactive Max quota; after that, a separate credit pool.

Important caveat: structured output. The 'anthropic' SDK path uses the tool-use trick for guaranteed Pydantic-validated output. 'claude -p' has no equivalent — must prompt for raw JSON and validate post-hoc, with a one-retry fallback for malformed responses.
design:
## Design sketch

### Backend selection

Add a new env var RECQUE_BACKEND that explicitly overrides auto-detection. Order of resolution:

1. Explicit: RECQUE_BACKEND=claude_cli|anthropic|openai|mock
2. Auto-detect: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY → anthropic, else OPENAI_API_KEY → openai, else mock
3. (claude_cli is never auto-selected — must be explicit, since 'claude' CLI being on PATH doesn't imply the user wants to use it)

### New backend method in recque_tui/core/ai_client.py

```python
import json, subprocess
from pydantic import ValidationError

CLAUDE_CLI_SYSTEM = '''Respond with a single JSON object that matches this schema. No prose, no markdown fences — JSON only.'''

def _generate_claude_cli(self, prompt: str, response_format: Type[T]) -> T:
    schema = response_format.model_json_schema()
    augmented = (
        f'{prompt}\n\n'
        f'{CLAUDE_CLI_SYSTEM}\n\n'
        f'Schema:\n{json.dumps(schema, indent=2)}'
    )

    for attempt in range(2):  # one retry on parse failure
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ['claude', '-p', augmented, '--output-format', 'json'],
                capture_output=True, text=True, check=True, timeout=60,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            raise RuntimeError('claude -p timed out after 60s')
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            raise RuntimeError(f'claude -p failed: {e.stderr}')

        # 'claude -p --output-format json' returns {'result': '...', ...}
        envelope = json.loads(result.stdout)
        payload_text = envelope.get('result', '').strip()

        # Strip any markdown fences the model might add despite instructions
        if payload_text.startswith('```'):
            payload_text = payload_text.split('\n', 1)[1].rsplit('```', 1)[0].strip()

        try:
            return response_format.model_validate_json(payload_text)
        except (ValidationError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e:
            if attempt == 0:
                logger.warning(f'claude -p returned invalid JSON, retrying: {e}')
                continue
            raise RuntimeError(f'claude -p returned non-conforming JSON after retry: {payload_text[:200]}')
```

### Wire into generate()

Add 'claude_cli' branch with the same fallback ladder as the others (try → log error → fall back to mock).

### Concurrency caveat

question_engine.py:prefetch_simpler_questions spawns N parallel calls. With claude_cli, that's N subprocess spawns at ~200-400ms each, each loading the Node.js binary. Cap concurrency at 2 for the claude_cli backend (or accept the latency).

### Detection / health check

On __init__, when backend=claude_cli is requested, verify 'claude' is on PATH ('shutil.which') and fail-fast with a friendly message if not. Don't try to verify auth — let the first call fail naturally.

### Config

Add to recque_tui/config.py: a 'backend' field on Config; honor env var RECQUE_BACKEND. Document in README and CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- New backend 'claude_cli' selectable via RECQUE_BACKEND env var
- Shells out to 'claude -p ... --output-format json' and parses the JSON envelope's 'result' field
- Uses prompt-engineered JSON output (with schema in prompt) since no tool-use is available
- One retry on parse failure before bubbling up
- Fallback ladder: claude_cli → mock (NOT to anthropic/openai — point is to avoid API spend)
- Concurrency cap to prevent N parallel subprocess spawns from question_engine.prefetch
- Friendly error when 'claude' is not on PATH
- AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md updated with how to use the dev-mode backend
- Tests: subprocess.run is mocked; verify schema is passed in prompt, JSON parsing handles both bare JSON and ```json fenced output, retry behavior
notes: User vision: 'while recque is running in dev, route question-generation through the Claude I am already paying for.' This is a personal-use case, single-user TUI, low volume — fits the allowed Agent SDK / claude -p usage pattern (it is the user's own auth, not a third-party login flow exposed to others).
close_reason: Implemented claude_cli backend with subprocess shim, schema-in-prompt JSON parsing, one-retry on malformed output, mock-only fallback, and 9 unit tests. Verified end-to-end against Max subscription: real Sonnet-generated Question returned and Pydantic-validated. Docs updated in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md.

title: Expand application service layer beyond SessionService
description: SessionService landed (recque_tui/application/session_service.py). Extend the application/ layer so question_screen.py becomes mostly presentation. Likely services: AnswerQuestionService (handle correct/incorrect, push/pop, prefetch), StartSessionService (skillmap + create_session), MasteryUpdateService. Screens call one service method per user action.
acceptance_criteria:
- New services live in recque_tui/application/
- question_screen.py no longer instantiates QuestionEngine directly for orchestration
- Each service has unit tests
- Mock generator path still works end-to-end

title: Promote LearningStack into a Session aggregate
description: Promote core/learning_stack.LearningStack into a full Session aggregate that owns: skills list, current_skill_index, stack, and per-attempt counters (questions_answered/correct). Expose answer(question, choice) -> AnswerOutcome and advance_skill() with invariants enforced inside the aggregate. UI screens should call aggregate methods rather than mutate fields directly.
acceptance_criteria:
- Session aggregate has typed methods answer() and advance_skill()
- Recursive push/pop logic moves from question_screen into the aggregate
- LearningStack.to_dict/from_dict shape preserved (no migration of session_progress.stack_state_json rows)
- ui/screens/question_screen.py shrinks materially (target: under ~250 lines)
- Existing tests still pass; new tests cover the aggregate's invariants

title: Remove _uncategorized placeholder logic from QuestionRepository.save
description: QuestionRepository.save invents a placeholder Topic named '_uncategorized' and a Skill under it when skill_id is not supplied. A repository making domain decisions. Move that responsibility to the caller (likely the new QuestionEngine/AnswerQuestionService path) and have the repository require a valid skill_id.
acceptance_criteria:
- QuestionRepository.save no longer creates Topic or Skill rows
- Callers pass a real skill_id (or service ensures one exists first)
- Mock generator and live engine both still cache questions correctly
- Existing test_repositories.py tests still pass (may need to update test setup to create a Skill first)

title: Map ORM rows to domain objects at the repository boundary
description: Today SessionRepository.get_paused/get_active return SQLAlchemy LearningSession instances that leak into UI code (e.g. ui/screens/question_screen.py:30). Map ORM rows to domain objects at the repository boundary so the UI never sees ORM types. Likely need: a domain Session dataclass (separate from the ORM LearningSession table).
acceptance_criteria:
- Repositories return domain objects, not ORM rows
- UI screens import from recque_tui.domain (or .application), not recque_tui.database.schema
- ORM types stay inside recque_tui/database/
